$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to artisanpack namespace; attributes/supports verbatim.

TypeScript port. Adds explicit 'wp-block-spacer' class to useBlockProps so the editor canvas matches frontend independent of __experimentalSelector internals. Upstream's `unlock(blockEditorPrivateApis)` swapped for a defensive accessor since the private-API key isn't exposed to fork consumers.
TypeScript port; class list adds 'wp-block-spacer' explicitly (parity with edit).
Single v1 entry ported verbatim (legacy numeric height/width → string px).
Upstream → core/separator transform preserved; added bidirectional block transforms for core/spacer ↔ artisanpack/spacer round-tripping.
TypeScript port of the ToolsPanel-based spacer controls; private-API access via defensive accessor instead of `unlock`.

Combined SCSS-to-CSS port. data-type selectors rewritten from core/spacer to artisanpack/spacer; Sass variables resolved ($grid-unit-10 → 8px, $black/$white → rgba); `.is-dark-theme &` parent selectors expanded.
Inline SVG for the inserter (editor doesn't bundle dashicons).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract. Upstream's PHP-side init wiring and example settings are not carried.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx + save.tsx add 'wp-block-spacer' to useBlockProps explicitly; upstream relies on __experimentalSelector to inject it.
edit.tsx + controls.tsx replace `unlock(privateApis).useSpacingSizes` with a defensive accessor — the fork can't import `../lock-unlock` from outside the block-library package.
transforms.ts adds bidirectional block transforms that don't exist upstream — required to coexist with core/spacer during the V2 rollout.
index.ts replaces upstream's PHP-side init wiring with the visual-editor auto-discovery contract; the `example` settings object isn't carried.
spacer.css inlines Sass color/grid variables rather than re-importing @wordpress/base-styles, and expands `.is-dark-theme &` to a flat selector list.
